title: Student charged in Danvers home invasion murder appears in court
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Anthony DeMayo, 18, arraigned on murder and home invasion charges in Essex Superior Court from Bridgewater State Hospital.
Judge ordered an independent competency evaluation for DeMayo, who was found incompetent to stand trial in April.
Janet Swallow, 68, was found murdered in her home; DeMayo was arrested nearby with a blood-stained knife.

title: Mass. Senate passes bill to protect immigrants, with notable differences from House version
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts Senate passed a bill limiting ICE activities, requiring compromise with the House's different version before Governor Healey's approval.
The Senate bill bans warrantless civil immigration arrests at sensitive locations and introduces civil liability for federal agents violating rights.
House version restricts warrantless arrests only in courthouses and includes rules for correctional facilities, missing in the Senate bill.

title: Kevin Garnett, Paul Pierce analyze what went wrong with ‘robotic’ Celtics offense
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kevin Garnett and Paul Pierce criticized the Boston Celtics' reliance on 3-point shots during 'KG Certified' podcast.
The Celtics were eliminated after losing the final three games against the 76ers, blowing a 3-1 series lead.
Pierce noted the impact of losing front-court players like Porzingis, Horford, affecting their offensive versatility.

title: Waiting in line in Boston is brutal. Some spots are still worth it.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Brookline Lunch in Cambridge is famous for its baklava pancakes, attracting customers willing to wait over an hour.
Cannonball Cafe in South Boston draws a younger crowd with good coffee and social media buzz, resulting in waits up to an hour.
Modern Pastry and Neptune Oyster in the North End are celebrated for their traditional offerings, with lines often exceeding an hour.

title: Mass. man wins $1M prize on lottery ticket bought at Kowloon
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Weiju Huang of Malden won a $1 million prize in the Massachusetts State Lottery's $4,000,000 Cash Doubler game.
Huang opted for a one-time cash payment, receiving $650,000 before taxes for his winning lottery ticket.
Kowloon Restaurant in Saugus, where the ticket was bought, receives a $10,000 bonus from the lottery.

title: Tom Brady trolls Kevin Hart with ‘newborn-sized’ jersey at Netflix roast
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Tom Brady made a surprise appearance at 'The Roast of Kevin Hart' on Netflix, delivering comedic jabs at the comedian.
Brady gifted Kevin Hart a kid-sized Knicks jersey after the 76ers' playoff loss, poking fun at Hart's height.
Brady compared his football successes to Hart's acting roles, highlighting his seven Super Bowl wins versus Hart's film work.

title: Are you traveling this summer? Tell us if your plans have changed.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: 24% of Americans reconsidered travel plans due to rising costs and global events, according to a poll by The Points Guy and YouGov.
Travel disruptions include closed airspace in the Middle East, cartel violence in Mexico, and a prolonged U.S. government shutdown.
Readers are invited to share how their summer travel plans have changed, with potential inclusion in a future Boston.com article.

title: NH authorities seeking ‘violent,’ ‘armed and dangerous’ fugitive
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Lisa Marie Lundstrom, 47, is wanted for absconding probation in New Hampshire and Massachusetts, deemed 'armed and dangerous.'
Lundstrom's criminal record includes charges of burglary, robbery, drug offenses, and assault dating back to 2007.
Authorities describe Lundstrom as 5'6", 220 pounds, with tattoos and piercings; public advised to contact 911 if seen.

title: Local obituary: Kai Prescott and Ella Brayton MacAusland, Wellesley siblings
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Kai Prescott and Ella Brayton MacAusland, ages 7 and 6, tragically passed away on April 24, 2026.
The siblings attended Schofield Elementary School in Wellesley, with services set at St. Andrew's Episcopal Church.
Donations in their memory can be made to The Schofield PTO, with details available online.

title: Fidelity reorganizes its workplace with new hires — and a few cuts
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Fidelity Investments is reorganizing and plans to hire thousands while cutting about 800 jobs, or 1% of its workforce.
The company is transitioning 25,000 roles to a new Technology and Product Operating model with 2,000 positions open.
Fidelity's restructuring aims to enhance skills for future growth and includes a full-time office return starting in September.
